Now deploy the AI assistant as REST-API so that it can be integrated into other applications. Here we deploy a REST-API on Cloud Foundry, but you can use any environment of your choice. In case you are new to Cloud Foundry, then Steps 1 and 2 of this Learning Journey get you started. The application that we deploy here consists of 4 files. The full code is available in this repository.  

  1. btpaiagent.py ➔ holds the actual application logic
  2. manifest.yaml ➔ contains for instance the memory requirements and environment variables
  3. requirements.txt ➔ lists the Python packages that will be installed
  4. runtime.txt ➔ specifies the Python version (python-3.12.x)

Before pushing the application to Cloud Foundry you need to make the following adjustments

  1. In btpaiagent.py specify your own SMTP server credentials to send emails. For productive use you may want to store these more securely, ie in the SAP Credential Store. An example is in this blog. 
  2. In manifest.yml enter your credentials for the SAP Generative AI Hub.

Then push your application to Cloud Foundry.

 

cf push

 

And you should see the URL of your REST-API!

 

Now an application can call that BTP AI agent. Let's test this from Python with a not so serious request. Let's use the AI agents functionality of getting the lunch menu and the TV program. Should I go for lunch or watch tv?

 

user_input = "Should I go for lunch or watch tv?"
backend_api = "REPLACEWITHYOURURL"
paylod = {'user_input': user_input}
headers = {'Accept' : 'application/json', 'Content-Type' : 'application/json'}
r = requests.get(backend_api, json=paylod, headers=headers, verify=False)
response = r.json()
btpaiagent_response = response['btpaiagent_response']
btpaiagent_response_log = response['btpaiagent_response_log']
print(btpaiagent_response)

 

And It's giving a good overview, especially when taking into account that both the lunch menu and the TV program were retrieved in German.

 

Put a sandbox user interface on top

To make all this more usable, here is a simple sandboxing user interface. For a productive project, you may want to use UI5 or SAP Build. This is also a Python application, now using the streamlit package for the user interface, deployed on Cloud Foundry. The full code is available in this repository.

  1. btpassistant.py ➔ holds the actual application logic and user interface
  2. manifest.yaml ➔ contains for instance the memory requirements and environment variables
  3. requirements.txt ➔ lists the Python packages that will be installed
  4. runtime.txt ➔ specifies the Python version (python-3.12.x)

Before pushing the application to Cloud Foundry you need to add the url of your own AI agent in btpassistant.py (the backend_api variable). Then push as usual to Cloud Foundry.

 

cf push

 

And this is the fruit of all the work. With a few basic tools the AI agent was able to let Peter know of the status of a specific invoice!

If you have fun with BTP AI agent concept, feel free to post here any tools / functions into the comments.

Happy agenting!

entry.title: Enhancing Business Agility with Enterprise Automation: SAP Build and SAP Signavio
entry.content:
The ability to intelligently automate business processes is top of mind for many stakeholders involved in process management across organizations. According to a recent IDC study, annual spending on Intelligent Process Automation (IPA) technologies is projected to reach $102 billion by 2028. Of this, $19.9 billion will be allocated to AI-powered automation tools, with an impressive annual growth rate of 43.4%.

While there will be certainly no shortage in resources to spend for automation software, the real challenge remains in the details: How can organizations implement a holistic approach for process excellence, which allows them to manage processes, identify bottlenecks, deliver scalable automations and integrate their process extension within a holistic process landscape. In the previous blogpost, @Manuel_Namyslo has already outlined in detail how SAP’s framework on Enterprise Automation delivers an solution to the problem statement mentioned above.

And this blogpost series will offer you a more detailed guidance on the interconnectivity of each service included in Enterprise Automation which are:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • and, of course, SAP Signavio.

Manuel has already published a blog on the joint usage patterns for SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in the first blogpost of the series.

Let’s explore the value you can unlock for your processes by combining SAP Build Process Automation with SAP Signavio in this one.

What is Enterprise Automation?

Enterprise automation with SAP software provides an integrated set of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and optimization of your processes and workflows. It helps you quickly and intuitively gain insights into your business processes and identify the right automation tool for the right outcome. While each solution can be leveraged independently, Enterprise Automation offer native integration between SAP Build, SAP integration Suite and SAP Signavio – supplemented with best practices, guidance and pre-build content which can be implemented directly.

  • SAP Signavio provides visibility into business processes and potential bottlenecks, root-cause analysis, and suggestions for improvement based on 200,000 industry benchmarks and best practices. This detailed understanding of inefficiencies across your organization enables you to optimize and automate processes.
  • SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform as a service with thousands of prebuilt integrations, APIs, events and connectors to SAP and third-party software applications. The suite reduces complexity across on-premise and multi-cloud environments, helping you optimize comprehensive business processes.
  • SAP Build to create automations and applications using the low-code and no-code design tools. Including pre-built automations and connectors for SAP software and third-party systems, the solutions let your business experts rapidly address process inefficiencies.

Leverage the combined power of SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Signavio

1. Process Recommendations

Every organization runs complex business processes, from procurement to order management and HR workflows. However, inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and compliance gaps often remain hidden within these processes, leading to delays and unnecessary costs.

SAP Signavio Process Insights is a lightweight solution designed to help businesses analyze and optimize their operational processes. It provides data-driven insights by identifying inefficiencies, automation opportunities, as well as performance bottlenecks, and can be leveraged alongside your SAP S/4HANA data. The tool enables companies to benchmark their processes against industry best practices, prioritize improvement initiatives, and accelerate digital transformation. By offering predefined process performance indicators and process recommendations, SAP Signavio Process Insights helps organizations enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and drive continuous improvement in their business operations.

However, identifying issues is just the first step—organizations need a way to act on these insights and implement improvements. This is where SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP Build come into play.

SAP Signavio Process Insights can recommend specific pre-built content, tailored to the process specifics. This content is available to be deployed with SAP Build Process Automation, so companies can directly implement these packages to make their scenarios executable.

2. From Insights to Action

Having the recommendations of what can be used is a great option to start. But there is more to the story. Organizations need a seamless way to make the outcomes of the process analysis actionable and continuously optimize their operations. This is where SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and SAP Build Process Automation come together to drive meaningful transformation.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence enables organizations to perform a full-fledged process mining, including:

  • Analysis process data extracted from SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, and other connected systems.
  • Detection of inefficiencies, such as approval delays, reworks needed or missed SLAs.
  • Visualization of process flows with its deviations, conformance and variants.

This insight provides a clear roadmap for improvement—but how do we act on it efficiently?

SAP Build Process Automation bridges the gap by enabling organizations to implement improvements with minimal IT effort.

Using the combination of the tools, businesses can define conditions and triggers based on findings from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and trigger an approval flow or a task automation scenario (e.g. an exception flow) directly using the native API connectivity.

For example, if Process Intelligence detects a high percentage of purchase orders stuck in approval, SAP Build Process Automation can automatically trigger a workflow that escalates delays, notifies the right stakeholders, or reroutes tasks to speed up processing.

Now, optimization is not a one-time effort—it requires continuous monitoring and refinement. Once automated workflows are in place, organizations must track their performance and fine-tune processes as needed.

With SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, businesses can:

  • Measure the impact of automation by tracking KPIs before and after implementation.
  • Identify new bottlenecks or areas for further automation in a cycle of continuous improvement, receiving the data from the executed workflow.
  • Refine processes dynamically, ensuring agility and efficiency in ever-changing business conditions.

3. Model-to-Execute: BPMN Import for Executable Processes

In today's dynamic business environment, organizations need to rapidly transform business processes from concept to execution. One of the key challenges has been the disconnect between process modeling and process automation—where business analysts model processes that then need to be separately implemented by technical teams, often leading to misalignment and delays.

SAP is looking to address this challenge through a BPMN Import capability, a roadmap item for SAP Build Process Automation, bridging the gap between process design and execution.

Traditionally, business processes follow this journey:

  1. Business analysts model best practice processes, what can be done using SAP Signavio Process Manager
  2. These models are handed off to technical teams
  3. Technical teams re-interpret and rebuild these models in execution platforms
  4. Discrepancies emerge between what was designed and what was implemented

This handoff creates friction, delays implementation, and often results in execution that doesn't fully align with the original vision.

The ability to import models from SAP Signavio Process Manager directly into SAP Build Process Automation transforms this approach. Now, the BPMN models created by business users can serve as the actual foundation for executable automation.

In addition, it is important to outline that SAP Build Process Automation will be supporting generic BPMN models import, allowing to upload BPMN diagrams from different solutions.

Please expect more details on this after the feature would be released.

Conclusion

The joint usage of SAP Signavio and SAP Build offers a powerful combination for businesses looking to optimize their processes and drive efficiency. By leveraging product recommendations, direct integration for automation, and model-to-execute capabilities, organizations can quickly identify, automate, and execute process improvements.

Please find some useful links below:

  • Official Enterprise Automation with SAP page 
  • How to get started with Enterprise Automation Guide 
  • Demo: Experience Enterprise Automation with SAP for Procure-to-Pay Processes
  • Whitepaper: Transition to SAP S/4HANA with Enterprise Automation  
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP Product Tour  
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP webinar

entry.title: Team Fight Detection: How Team Liquid Transformed Their Analysis Process
entry.content:
Competitive esports is a field, where speed and precision decide between winning and losing. Analysts and players typically have the daunting task of sifting through hours of game play to get valuable insights that can change the course of a match. Team Liquid, one of the world's most successful esports organizations, is always seeking new methods to achieve that competitive edge. In the Next Level Esports Center on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), Team Liquid can analyze various aspects of all pro-games in an effort to prepare for their next League of Legends match-up and it already helped them to win a number of championships in the past years.

In the past it was not feasible to detect teamfights, since it’s a time-consuming task to analyze hundreds of matches every month. That's why the team required a solution to automate the process of team fight detection in their League of Legends matches.

This article discusses how Team Liquid uses SAP to automate and optimize their team fight detection process, saving time, minimizing costs, and having improved insights into their gameplay.

 

The Challenge: Manual Analysis in a Fast-Paced World

In traditional analysis, identifying and studying team fights — the pivotal moments in a match when two teams clash — is a labor-intensive process. Analysts were required to manually watch every fight, keeping track of vital information, such as which players took part, the duration of the fight, and how the fight impacted the game overall. Because one game can last between 25 and 45 minutes, this process could consume hours of analysts' time, with the outcome being prone to human error.

For a high-performance team like Team Liquid, this inefficiency was a major issue. They needed a more precise and automated system that would quickly detect team fights and provide valuable insights for coaching and strategy.

 

The Solution: Automated Team Fight Detection with SAP

The solution designed for Team Liquid is based on an automated team fight detection system that works very harmoniously with the existing tools on SAP BTP. The system works by analyzing in-game data for specific events, such as damage dealt, player proximity, and the timing of interactions. A range of machine learning models were trained to evaluate their performance with respect to the rule-based model. Using the resulting feature importances together with manual parameter optimization allowed the rule-based system to perform on par with the machine learning models. By defining a series of conditions, the system can automatically identify when a team fight begins and ends — no manual tracking necessary.

The key technology enabler behind this solution is SAP HANA, which provides the computational power needed to process and handle enormous volumes of data in real time. Using the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) of HANA Cloud, the team was able to implement advanced clustering algorithms, which dynamically group players based on how close they are to one another and their participation in a team fight.  The different clustering methods and their parameters were evaluated using PAL's slight silhouette score and Team Liquids’ expert game knowledge. This combination of methods ensures that all the team fights, no matter how complex or dynamic, are captured.

Additionally, the solution utilizes machine learning algorithms to improve the detection process over time, continually increasingits speed and accuracy. By detecting players movements and interactions, the system is able to track team fights across multiple matches, ensuring that analysts can quickly identify and examine critical moments without having to manually comb through dozens of hours of gameplay.

 

Key Benefits for Team Liquid

The impact of this automated team fight detection system has been game-changing for Team Liquid, both in terms of time savings and cost reductions. The team estimates that the new system saves 10,000 working hours annually — time that was previously spent manually identifying team fights, a task which is now completely automated. This time-saving benefit allows analysts to focus on strategically more relevant tasks, such as strategy development and player performance analysis, ultimately enhancing the team’s competitive edge.

From a financial perspective, the system is expected to save $250,000 annually by reducing the time spent for manual analysis. With saved time and cost savings, Team Liquid can shift resources to other resources, such as training players, scouting, or equipment, all of which support their continuous success in the extremely competitive arena of esports.

 

Real-World Impact: Actionable Insights for Coaches and Analysts

While the ability of the system to save time and expense is impressive, the real value lies in the actionable insights it provides to Team Liquid’s coaches and analysts. By allowing the team to automatically detect team fights, the team has better and more stable data available that can be used immediately to improve their decision-making process.

For instance, coaches can now quickly see which players were involved in specific team fights and how those fights impacted the game. They are now able to monitor player performance within such key situations and identify the areas that need to be re-evaluated and improved. With 16.000 analyzed pro-games in the database, this is helping them to recognize patterns in their own and opponents’ behavior to make respective adaptations to their strategy.

 

Conclusion

The team fight detection feature developed for Team Liquid using SAP HANA Cloud represents another milestone in the long-standing co-innovation partnership. By automating the time-consuming process of detecting team fights, Team Liquid has saved thousands of hours, reduced costs, and gained invaluable insights into their gameplay. This solution has empowered the team to focus on what matters most — improving their performance and strategy, both individually and as a team.

As esports continues to grow and evolve, leveraging machine learning and artificial intelligence with SAP products will be key to staying competitive. For Team Liquid, this new system is not just about saving time — it’s about using data to drive better decisions and ultimately achieve success in the world’s most competitive gaming tournaments.

Big thank you to @Thomas_Woelkhart for the design and the development of the feature together with our partner Team Liquid.

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 10: BTP Cloud Foundry Spaces
entry.content:
In this episode, we explore the concept of spaces within SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP). Spaces allow for organized development environments within sub-accounts, enabling selective access to applications, such as HR or finance, ensuring sensitive data is secured. We differentiate spaces from subaccounts, focusing on the benefits of spaces for project management and access control. Additionally, we highlight the importance of memory quota management for spaces, with a look ahead to further exploration of quota configurations in the next episode. Spaces offer a streamlined way to manage resources and permissions efficiently.

 

Takeaways

  • Spaces in BTP allow additional organizational levels for Cloud Foundry runtime in subaccounts.
  • Cloud Foundry runtime offers the ability to create spaces for specific project deployments.
  • Developers can be given restricted access to specific spaces, ensuring security for sensitive applications.
  •  The main difference between a space and a subaccount is that business users are associated with subaccounts, not spaces.
  • In scenarios where identifying different business users is needed, a new subaccount is required.
  • If business user identification is not necessary, adding an additional space is recommended.
  • The same Cloud connector can be used in multiple spaces, benefiting from the same configuration in the sub-account.
  • Roles and trust configurations are managed at the subaccount level, not the space level.
  • Spaces require configuration of quotas, including memory allocation for Cloud Foundry.
  • Quotas in spaces provide full control over the memory consumed by Cloud Foundry in each space.
  • The next episode will dive deeper into how to assign quotas for better resource management.

entry.title: New Learning Journey Extending SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 using SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello SAP CX Community! This is Fernando speaking!

During the last few years, we've been producing learning assets for SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 1 extensibility topics. This year though we plan to focus on version 2 extensibility and integration topics. So, today we're very proud to announce in this blogpost, the next learning milestone for those who want to master the extensibility of SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2 using SAP Build Apps.

Please join me in welcoming to our SAP's learning platform, this new learning journey: 


New Learning Journey 
Extending SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 using SAP Build Apps

If you need to get ready to extend SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2 using SAP Build Apps as a citizen developer, you’ve come to the right place. You'll grow the hands-on knowledge that will help you to run end to end the extensibility project. And there is a plus, you'll also learn how to include generative AI capabilities from SAP Core AI in this course.

We've curated our content so that after completing this learning journey, developers, administrators, and consultants will gain the skills to be able to setup, configure and implement the extensibility between SAP SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2 using SAP Build Apps and SAP Core AI.

Here you have a glimpse of how it looks:

 

Audience
The course is meant to be helpful for:

  • Technical Consultants
  • Administrators
  • Developers
  • Architects

Learning Objectives
Upon the completion of this course, a Consultant or Developer will be able to extend SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 using SAP Build Apps and SAP AI Core Services to customize the solution to their specific business needs.

Learning Content
The learning journey consists of 3 units and provides 3 hours of content:

  • Extending SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 with SAP Build Apps: An introduction to the features and limitations of Side-by-Side extensibility and how to establish the connection between SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 and SAP Build Apps.
  • Designing an Extension App with SAP Build Apps to Extend SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
    This unit guides you:
    • Create a new project in SAP Build Apps.
    • Design and preview the user interface of the extension app.
    • Integrate the data from SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 with the connected extension app.
    • Integrate the SAP AI Core service with SAP Build Apps to enable the AI-based generation and translation of e-mail texts in the extension app.
    • Deploy the Extension App.
  • Embedding and Testing the Extension App in SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
    This unit walks you through:
    • Create a mashup to display the extension app within the SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 user interface.
    • Log into SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 to ensure the embedded app works as expected.

After completing successfully this Learning Journey you'll be granted with a digital badge like this one which could be shared and linked to your social networks:

 

 

 

 

 

Give it a try! We plan to create more and more extensibility learning content for SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2, and we'd like you to go over the lessons and let us know if they are useful for you or your team.

Additional Resources
There is a related live session for In-App extensibility for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud version 2. You can find more free learning journeys at the SAP Learning site for Integration and Extesibility, where you can build in-demand SAP skills for variety of topics specifically the SAP CX Solutions portfolio. If you are more in for quick video learning, then, look for our videos Integration and Extensibility videos for SAP Customer Experience to get updates on the latest integration and extensibility videos for SAP Customer Experience. solutions.

entry.title: How to Enable Joule Capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
SAP recently announced some new Joule capabilities for developers within SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps. If you’re wondering how to enable these capabilities in your workspace, you’re in the right place.

In this post, we’ll look at the steps to undertake to enable the AI capabilities. But first, let’s just recap the announcement and the various recent posts:

  • Joule for developers, announced on March 18, is a set of AI capabilities enabled by Joule across SAP Build and ABAP developer tools.
  • Read the announcement blog here
  • Read the technical blog here
  • Read the FAQs on Joule for developer here

Now, lets look at the steps to enable the AI capabilities in SAP Build:

1. On the Build homepage, click Control Tower in the left-hand navigation panel.

2. Select Generative AI from the settings canvas.

3. Toggle the Enable AI button

 

4. Read and accept the SAP AI terms

5. Verify that the AI features are enabled

Availability and Licensing

Joule capabilities for developers are currently available across SAP Build Code, ABAP, SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Apps.

Good news! Now through the end of June, we’re making Joule capabilities for developers accessible for free to all developers using SAP Build and ABAP Development Tools. For all SAP Build development tools, this free period extends until the end of June 2025. The ABAP AI capabilities will be available for free until August 2025. The only requirement to take advantage of this free offer is for the organization/user to sign the SAP AI Terms. Once this free offer concludes, the commercial model based on AI Units will take effect.

Note: You will still need the license to use the underlying SAP Build solutions and ABAP to utilize the AI capabilities within those tools.

Start leveraging the power of AI today! Enable Joule for developers in your SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps environments and experience a significant boost in your development efficiency. Don't miss out on the free offer available for a limited time!

entry.title: SAP BTP Developer's Guide: Now Featuring CAP Java Tutorials!
entry.content:
Great news for Java developers!  The SAP BTP Developer's Guide now features hands-on tutorials for CAP Java-based incident management.

With the latest update, you’ll learn how to build, deploy, and optimize incident management apps using CAP Java—following SAP BTP best practices. Time to level up your skills and ship better apps!

Tutorials : Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application Following SAP BTP Developer’s Guide 
Github: Incident Management Java

Following the tutorials you will learn the following:

  • How to create a CAP project
  • How to add a domain model
  • How to create services
  • How to add data to your database

Domain Model of the application: 

using { cuid, managed, sap.common.CodeList } from '@sap/cds/common';
namespace sap.capire.incidents; 

/**
* Incidents created by Customers.
*/
entity Incidents : cuid, managed {  
customer     : Association to Customers;
title        : String  @title : 'Title';
urgency        : Association to Urgency default 'M';
status         : Association to Status default 'N';
conversation  : Composition of many {
    key ID    : UUID;
    timestamp : type of managed:createdAt;
    author    : type of managed:createdBy;
    message   : String;
};
}

/**
* Customers entitled to create support Incidents.
*/
entity Customers : managed { 
key ID        : String;
firstName     : String;
lastName      : String;
name          : String = firstName ||' '|| lastName;
email         : EMailAddress;
phone         : PhoneNumber;
incidents     : Association to many Incidents on incidents.customer = $self;
creditCardNo  : String(16) @assert.format: '^[1-9]\d{15}$';
addresses     : Composition of many Addresses on addresses.customer = $self;
}

entity Addresses : cuid, managed {
customer      : Association to Customers;
city          : String;
postCode      : String;
streetAddress : String;
}

entity Status : CodeList {
key code: String enum {
    new = 'N';
    assigned = 'A'; 
    in_process = 'I'; 
    on_hold = 'H'; 
    resolved = 'R'; 
    closed = 'C'; 
};
criticality : Integer;
}

entity Urgency : CodeList {
key code: String enum {
    high = 'H';
    medium = 'M'; 
    low = 'L'; 
};
}

type EMailAddress : String;
type PhoneNumber : String;

Here's some code snippet from the JAVA custom handler: 

package customer.incident_management.handler;

import cds.gen.processorservice.Incidents;
import cds.gen.processorservice.ProcessorService_;
import cds.gen.sap.capire.incidents.*;
import com.sap.cds.ql.Select;
import com.sap.cds.services.ErrorStatuses;
import com.sap.cds.services.ServiceException;
import com.sap.cds.services.cds.CqnService;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.EventHandler;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.annotations.Before;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.annotations.ServiceName;
import com.sap.cds.services.persistence.PersistenceService;
import org.slf4j.Logger;
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
import org.springframework.stereotype.Component;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.Locale;

@Component
@ServiceName(ProcessorService_.CDS_NAME)
public class ProcessorServiceHandler implements EventHandler {

    private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ProcessorServiceHandler.class);

    private final PersistenceService db;

    public ProcessorServiceHandler(PersistenceService db) {
        this.db = db;
    }

    /*
     * Change the urgency of an incident to "high" if the title contains the word "urgent"
     */
    @Before(event = CqnService.EVENT_CREATE)
    public void ensureHighUrgencyForIncidentsWithUrgentInTitle(List<Incidents> incidents) {
        for (Incidents incident : incidents) {
            if (incident.getTitle().toLowerCase(Locale.ENGLISH).contains("urgent") &&
                    incident.getUrgencyCode() == null || !incident.getUrgencyCode().equals("H")) {
                incident.setUrgencyCode("H");
                logger.info("Adjusted Urgency for incident '{}' to 'HIGH'.", incident.getTitle());
            }

        }
    }

    /*
     * Handler to avoid updating a "closed" incident
     */
    @Before(event = CqnService.EVENT_UPDATE)
    public void ensureNoUpdateOnClosedIncidents(Incidents incident) {
        Incidents in = db.run(Select.from(Incidents_.class).where(i -> i.ID().eq(incident.getId()))).single(Incidents.class);
        if (in.getStatusCode().equals("C")) {
            throw new ServiceException(ErrorStatuses.CONFLICT, "Can't modify a closed incident");
        }

    }

}

This is your cue to clone the repo or dive into the tutorial and discover the elegance of CAP Java!

Once your application is ready, level up your development workflow by learning how to set up a robust CI/CD pipeline. This will help you automate testing, deployment, and delivery, ensuring smooth and efficient software releases!

The next part: Set Up a CI/CD Pipeline for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime . 

Keep Learning!

Java  SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  SAP Business Application Studio

entry.title: SAP BTP Empowers Customers and Partners to Unlock Business Potential
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is transforming how businesses operate by providing powerful, AI-driven tools for development, automation, integration, data management, and analytics—across both SAP and non-SAP applications. With SAP BTP, businesses can harness the power of generative AI to streamline processes and innovate more effectively.

At SAPinsider EMEA 2024 in Copenhagen, Denmark, we had the opportunity to meet with some of the most innovative SAP BTP customers and partners. Their success stories showcase how SAP BTP is helping businesses break new ground with solutions like SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite. Here’s a look at some of these incredible stories.

SAP BTP Customers

  • Open Grid Europe (OGE) sped up HR process by 50% and improved invoice process efficiency by building applications with SAP BTP, using SAP Build.
  • Floralux unlocked 40% total cost of ownership (TCO) savings while gaining a global view of their data and customer insights with SAP BTP, using SAP Integration Suite.
  • Norsk Hydro accelerated development time, reduced data errors, and improved decision-making capabilities with the power of SAP BTP.
  • Girbau modernized its analytics by providing a single source of truth to expedite decision-making with SAP BTP.

SAP BTP Partners

  • SEIDOR empowers customers to stay ahead of the competition by innovating, extending, and integrating their data with SAP BTP.
  • REPLY brings together their strategic, creative, and innovative approach to drive transformative results with SAP BTP.
  • Avvale is driving modern business evolution through the circular economy with SAP BTP.

Discover more about SAP BTP including SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite.

Explore additional inspiring SAP BTP partner stories.

If you’re an SAP partner, access a wealth of exclusive resources on SAP PartnerEdge to further accelerate your growth and success with SAP BTP.
